_meta.source: BOT BEHAVIOUR §1 universal red-flag override (verbatim §1 positives from medical_e3_recall.json) + word-order/proximity variants required to fire the verbatim escalation trace and its jaw-less form
_meta.status: INTERIM harm-floor list. Poor paraphrase recall BY DESIGN. Not coverage. Full E3 detector gates on medical_e3_recall.json.
_meta.arabic: INTERIM native Arabic layer (#329, 2026-07-16): transcription of the spec §1 descriptor classes (crushing/squeezing/stabbing/searing chest pain, radiation to arm/jaw/back, one-sided numbness) into MSA. Safety-floor, NOT coverage. Colloquial Gulf dialect, code-switched, and full symptom vocabulary are the clinician-authored Tier-2 follow-up. Fires against `raw` (safety_check.py:286 passes both message_en and raw), bypassing the lossy AR->EN translation that caused the live prod bypass.
_meta.clinical_signoff: PENDING — EN: Q1-triggers ratification; AR interim: EXPEDITED tick on the descriptor-transcription list (#329 Tier-1). Ships under the safety exception; Tier-2 full AR vocabulary follows.
_meta.match_field: entries default to literal (re.escape) substring; entries with match:"regex" are compiled as regex.
_meta.addendum_2026-07-16: Post-audit spec-conformance ADDENDUM (SEPARATE clinician tick-line): the spec classifies REAL inability to breathe (L216, distinct from panic/shallow breathlessness which L102 excludes) and FAINTING (L148) as red flags — both were MISSING from the EN base list too (predates #329; tracked in the EN spec-gap ticket). Added the clearest doc-cited real-inability + fainting phrases, EN+AR. The real-vs-shallow breathlessness DISCRIMINATOR (phrase boundary between excluded panic breathlessness and mandated real inability) is a clinical-linguistic call left as a BLANK for clinician judgment, NOT engineering-authored.
